title: crepis praemorsa subsp. dinarica
intro: Crepis praemorsa subsp. dinarica, also known as Dinaric Hawk's Beard or Dinaric Crepis, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving liver function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Crepis praemorsa subsp. dinarica is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its roots are sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
